file_insights.purpose: Sealed class for type-safe synchronization state representation

key_components.name: SyncState
key_components.responsibility: Root type for all sync states

key_components.name: Offline
key_components.responsibility: Represents offline state with pending operations count

key_components.name: Syncing
key_components.responsibility: Represents active sync with optional progress tracking

patterns_discovered.description: Using Kotlin sealed classes to represent mutually exclusive states with type safety
patterns_discovered.benefit: Compiler enforces exhaustive when statements, preventing missing state handlers

patterns_discovered.description: Syncing state includes optional progress to support both determinate and indeterminate progress
patterns_discovered.benefit: Flexibility for different sync scenarios without creating separate state classes

patterns_discovered.description: Using Kotlin data classes for states that carry relevant metadata
patterns_discovered.benefit: Automatic equality, toString, copy, and destructuring support

patterns_discovered.description: Comprehensive documentation for sealed class and each subtype
patterns_discovered.benefit: Clear intent and usage guidance for consumers of the state

gotchas_discovered.description: The progress parameter in Syncing is nullable with default null, making it indeterminate by default
gotchas_discovered.risk: Consumers might assume progress is always a value and crash on null without proper handling
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Documentation indicates null means indeterminate, but consumers should add defensive null checks

gotchas_discovered.description: Offline state accepts any Int for pendingCount without validation
gotchas_discovered.risk: Negative pending counts could be set, creating invalid state
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Consider adding require() or similar validation in constructor

approach_outcome.quality: High - follows Kotlin conventions and best practices
approach_outcome.architecture_fit: Excellent - sealed class provides type-safe state representation suitable for UI driving
approach_outcome.effort_efficiency: Optimal - simple, single-file implementation without unnecessary complexity

recommendations.suggestion: Add validation to Offline constructor to ensure pendingCount >= 0
recommendations.rationale: Prevents creation of semantically invalid states
recommendations.implementation: Add 'require(pendingCount >= 0)' in Offline.init or as primary constructor requirement

recommendations.suggestion: Add extension functions for state type checking
recommendations.rationale: Improves readability of state checks in consuming code

recommendations.suggestion: Consider adding timestamp to Syncing state
recommendations.rationale: Enables tracking sync duration and timing-related UI features

recommendations.suggestion: Add example usage in KDoc for the sealed class
recommendations.rationale: Clarifies how to consume state in when expressions
